description: wifi is a Python library providing WiFi management functionality (scan, connect, disconnect) on Linux via wpa_supplicant.

documentation.example: from wifi import Cell; print([c.ssid for c in Cell.all('wlan0')])
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/wifi && python3 wifi.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/wifi && python3 wifi.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/wifi && python3 wifi.py --help`. WiFi management (scan/connect) on Linux.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz] in the wifi category. Fits when the engagement needs: WiFi management (scan/connect) on Linux.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: WiFi management (scan/connect) on Linux.

metadata.example: from wifi import Cell; print([c.ssid for c in Cell.all('wlan0')])
